questions.question: Define left coset and right coset of a subgroup H in a group G. Are they always equal?
keyPoints: Define left coset aH = {ah : h ∈ H}
Define right coset Ha = {ha : h ∈ H}
They are equal if H is normal in G
They may differ if H is not normal

questions.question: State and prove Lagrange's Theorem. If |G| = 12 and |H| = 3, find the number of distinct left cosets of H in G.
Prove that cosets of a subgroup H in G partition the group G.
keyPoints: Show every element belongs to some coset
Show any two cosets are either equal or disjoint

questions.question: Define normal subgroup. Give an example of a normal subgroup and a non-normal subgroup in S₃.
keyPoints: Define normal: aN = Na for all a ∈ G
Verify left ≠ right cosets for non-normal case

questions.question: Explain the First Isomorphism Theorem with an example. Use φ: Z → Zₙ defined by φ(x) = x mod n.
In the dihedral group D₄, find the index of the rotation subgroup R = {e, r, r², r³} and verify it is normal.
keyPoints: Show aR = Ra for all a ∈ D₄
List left and right cosets to verify equality

questions.question: What is the kernel of the homomorphism φ: S₄ → S₃ induced by action on 3 points? Find the order of the kernel.
keyPoints: This is isomorphic to S₁ × S₁
Actually kernel ≅ S₂ for each fixed point set
Wait: action on 3 from 4 gives kernel of order 6 (isomorphic to S₃)

questions.question: Let G = Z₂₀ and H = ⟨5⟩ = {0, 5, 10, 15}. (a) Find all distinct left cosets of H in G. (b) Find the index [G:H]. (c) Is H normal in G? Justify. (d) Construct the quotient group G/H.
keyPoints: G is abelian so H is normal
G/H ≅ Z₅ with elements as coset representatives

questions.question: (a) Prove that if H is a subgroup of index 2 in G, then H is normal in G. (b) In S₄, consider H = {e, (12), (34), (12)(34)}. Find the index [S₄ : H] and determine if H is normal. (c) Find the number of elements of order 2 in S₄ that lie in H.
keyPoints: Index 2: only two cosets, H and G-H, must equal
H is not normal in S₄ (conjugates change pair structure)

questions.question: Consider the group homomorphism φ: Z₆ → Z₃ defined by φ([x]₆) = [x]₃. (a) Find ker(φ). (b) Prove ker(φ) is normal. (c) Apply First Isomorphism Theorem to find Z₆/ker(φ). (d) How many cosets are there?
keyPoints: Kernel is always normal by homomorphism property

questions.question: Let G = S₃ and N = {e, (123), (132)}. (a) List all left cosets and all right cosets of N in G. (b) Is N normal? (c) Construct the multiplication table for G/N. (d) What is the isomorphism type of G/N?
keyPoints: Yes, N is normal in S₃ (index 2)
